steps: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your core engaged, back flat, and neck neutral.
Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor.
Push back up to the starting position.
cues: Keep elbows tucked at a 45-degree angle.
Squeeze your glutes and brace your core.
mistakes: Sagging hips (hyperextending the lower back).
Flaring elbows straight out to the sides.

steps: Lie flat on a bench holding two dumbbells at chest level.
Plant your feet firmly on the ground.
Press the dumbbells straight up until your arms are fully extended.
Lower them slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Keep your wrists straight.
mistakes: Bouncing the weights off the chest.
Lifting your glutes off the bench.

steps: Lie on the bench with your eyes under the bar.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar and lower it to your mid-chest.
Press the bar back up in a slight arc toward your shoulders.
cues: Drive through your heels.
Pull the bar apart to engage your lats.
mistakes: Flaring the elbows.
Pressing the bar straight up instead of in an arc.

steps: Grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Hang with your arms fully extended and core engaged.
Pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar.
Lower yourself slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Drive your elbows down to the floor.
Keep your chest up.
mistakes: Using momentum (swinging/kipping) to get up.
Not completing the full range of motion.

steps: Sit at the pulldown machine and adjust the knee pad.
Grip the bar wider than shoulder-width.
Pull the bar down toward your upper chest.
Slowly return the bar to the starting position.
cues: Lean back slightly (about 10-15 degrees).
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom.
mistakes: Pulling the bar behind the neck.
Using your lower back momentum.

steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip.
Hinge at the hips, keeping your back straight and nearly parallel to the floor.
Row the barbell toward your lower chest/upper stomach.
Lower the bar under control.
cues: Pull your elbows straight back.
Keep your chest up and lower back neutral.
mistakes: Jerking the upper body up to move the weight.
Rounding the lower back.

steps: Stand holding a barbell or dumbbells at shoulder height.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
Press the weight straight up overhead until your arms are extended.
Lower the weight back to the starting position.
cues: Push your head through the 'window' of your arms at the top.
Keep your wrists stacked over your elbows.
mistakes: Leaning back excessively.
Pushing the bar out in front of you instead of straight up.

steps: Place the barbell across your upper back and unrack it.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider.
Push your hips back and bend your knees to lower into a squat.
Drive through your mid-foot to stand back up.
cues: Keep your chest up and core braced.
Push your knees outward in the direction of your toes.
mistakes: Letting your knees cave inward.
Rising with your hips faster than your shoulders (good morning squat).

steps: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell vertically against your chest.
Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower your hips down and back until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive back to the starting position.
cues: Keep your elbows tucked inside your knees at the bottom.
Keep the weight close to your body.
mistakes: Letting the weight pull your upper body forward.
Not going deep enough.

steps: Hold a barbell or dumbbells in front of your thighs.
Hinge at your hips, pushing them back while keeping your legs mostly straight (slight bend in the knee).
Lower the weight until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings.
Squeeze your glutes to return to a standing position.
cues: Keep the bar/dumbbells dragging closely along your legs.
Maintain a flat, neutral back.
mistakes: Rounding the lower back.
Squatting the weight down instead of hinging.

steps: Stand holding dumbbells with your palms facing forward.
Keep your upper arms stationary against your sides.
Curl the weights up toward your shoulders by flexing your elbows.
Lower the weights slowly.
cues: Squeeze your biceps at the top.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase.
mistakes: Swinging your body to lift the weight.
Letting your elbows drift forward.

steps: Attach a rope or straight bar to a high cable pulley.
Grip the attachment and tuck your elbows to your sides.
Push the attachment down until your arms are fully extended.
Return slowly to the starting position.
cues: Keep your elbows pinned to your ribs.
Spread the rope apart at the bottom for an extra squeeze.
mistakes: Letting your elbows flare or move forward.
Using your body weight to push down.

steps: Start on your forearms and toes, with your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels.
Engage your core, glutes, and quads.
Hold the position for the specified duration.
cues: Pull your belly button toward your spine.
Push the floor away with your forearms.
mistakes: Sagging your hips toward the floor.
Hiking your hips high into the air.

steps: Attach a rope to a cable pulley set at upper-chest height.
Grip the rope from underneath and step back.
Pull the rope toward your face, letting your elbows flare high and wide.
Squeeze your upper back, then slowly return.
cues: Imagine hitting a back double bicep pose.
Pull the center of the rope to your nose.
mistakes: Pulling the rope down toward your chest.
Using too much weight and leaning back.
